item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image came from here and is All Rights Reserved, and the second image could not be sourced, leaving it to be assumed as non-compliant. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fits Wondertainment perfecly! Completely harmless if used correctly, altough using it in a wrong way will most likely go wrong (but then again, most things are like this anyway)… Loved the "May contain nuts" at the end, it also kinda gives an idea of how these things are made (Wondertainment kidnapping and murdering people does not fit my headcanon, but I believe he/she/it/they would cultivate these in a lab).
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All I can think of right now is "I really hope Dad doesn't borrow the tube of Wonderlube for a 'wrestling match' with Mom…. The image this gives is rather horrifying.

This has been an announcement from your local SCPerv.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That face looks like "Boy if you don't get that camera out of my face" or "Now, how did I get packaged and sold to kids?"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) I understand it as a lube in the sense of SCP-170 (Crosslink team! Found one for ya'll!)
2) Palm first seems to be the implication. If you've ever seen or had a superglue accident, that's generally how it goes.
3) Actually Blood Plasma is both viscous (like unseparated blood) and yellow-green. You might be thinking of Blood Serum, which has had the mucous-like clotting factors removed, leaving a thin, yellow fluid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some issues:

  1. This is an adhesive. It's glue. The opposite of lube.
  2. A gas leak to explain a few cuts on his face? Seems like that would do a lot more damage. Were they fused palm-first or something?
  3. Blood plasma isn't viscous. Or green, for that matter.

Still, great article. Upvoted.

item.title: Explanation
item.content:encoded: No, they removed the extra fingers and separated the boy's hands from his face. The scarring was explained with a gas leak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have no clue how the Foundation can explain off extra fingers and face hands with a gas leak, but okay.

Pictures are creepy, and the tone is spot-on. Easily an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"May contain traces of nuts!"

I died of laughter :'D God Im alone at home… xDD
+1 :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wondertainment does children's toys. Not adult toys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this is what got me to upvote too. Little things like this really do effect the quality of the entire work sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Warning: May contain traces of nuts!

Instant upvote. I'd upvote the Kit too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could multiple living organisms be attached together by 1103-2?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Doctor Wondertainment always providing new things for the little children to do, too bad the Foundation has to ruin all the fun.

Great work! The article was very unique and interesting, as well as humorous (I liked the bit about the nuts).

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wait can we perform a frisky face operation on scp-096 and make it so that looking at the 1103 face would trigger his reaction?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for 'may contain nuts'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well…this put me off my lunch. +1 and go Wondertainment.

Also, "may contain traces of nuts" put a smile on my face, and the conflict of scary/silly emotions that you get from reading this page make it all the more effective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's creepy, it's cool, it's funny - it's Wondertainment! - and I want one.

Best Wondertainment yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm in the middle of class now and struggling to suppress a laugh because of this.
Yay. Congrats!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty well descripted, I see nothing wrong with the description and the Recovery Log is excellent. Definitely upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gah. The face was a mindscrew moment for me. I swear the eyes were following me. That just made the rest of the entry all the creepier, imagining adding an extra head to my body and watching it take over…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the compliments!

Now.
1) Yes. That's how the Human Construction Set works.

2) No, This works on living cellular tissues only.

3) No. The cells have to be alive. If you recall the transplants sold by Dr Wonderainment have somehow been treated so that their cells don't die if refrigerated at times that a normal transplant wouldn't be able to endure, among other things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for the creepiness of this idea and the brilliant job done writing it.

Also a few questions.

1. Can you assemble a creature entirely from these things. Maybe use a slug in the center, but cover it all in grafted bits.

2. Can you use mechanical parts with this SCP. What would happen if you took a Class-D who had lost an arm or something when SCP-██ attacked, a robotic arm and a bit of wonderlube and stuck them to each other. Or stuck loads of muscle tissue together over a metal framework as bones. Or spread a layer of wonderlube over a Class-D's arm and pushed a marble into it, then let it harden.

3. If someone cut up a dead Class-D and stuck them back together with wonderlube would it bring them back to life?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Will someone please give me an amnesiac? I really, really wanna get rid of this mental image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, good Lord, I just thought of what would happen if someone mistook Wonderlube for regular lube.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for spotting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
labelled SCP-1103-1-1 to SCP-1103-1-11

I think you meant "SCP-1103-1 to SCP-1103-11"

I think you labeled the packaged head wrong, actually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not usually a Wondertainment fan, but this one is brilliant. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Wonderlube. Wow. That did it for me. That and the meat-packed face. Pretty well written too, so upvote for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was an excellent choice!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*shrug* Can't please everyone.
Asides from the human construction set (ever played with LEGO in unintended ways? This takes it to 11) the only scary bit here is where does Wondertainment get the body parts. They look uniform because of processing but have differing ethnicity, gender, that's as far as DNA can tell us. Meaning they aren't likely lab cultivated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's really creative but for me there's no hook. Nothing especially spooky or funny or whatever… It's just creative. no-vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, I have seen them used this way in another Wondertainment article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I strongly recommend removing the (R) symbols, because they're too much on the side of Wondertainment-as-corporation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Replaced the picture with an actual Funny Finger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second that. Great article, great first picture, but the earmouse is way too easily recognizable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted just for the Instructional Leaflet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And now i'm thinking SCP-1103-1-10 and SCP-1103-1-11 to be Perky Penis® and Vigorous Vulva®.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No problem. That's why I joined. I have no sense of humor, so I can see stuff like that.

First thought I had when I read this: Real-life futanari. There's something wrong with me.

Unless Wondertainment doesn't deal in sexual organs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks!
That's what you get when someone is posting an SCP without proofreading, and when everyone is too busy snorting beverages out of their noses to fix the error.

See, 1043 is also my work, and the number kinda crept into my stupid head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How'd we go from SCP-1103 to SCP-1043?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Just like with traffic lights, wait for the green light!"

Perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My boss asked what the *thud* he just heard was, and I had to come up with some other reason for why my forehead would have suddenly come into contact with my desk XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This just hits the Wondertainment feel perfectly. Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't stop laughing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, you know any image that does the same job, but is (less known)/montage?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… I'm sorry it really doesn't work.

Look. Google for "ear mouse": https://www.google.com/search?client=ubuntu&channel=fs&q=mouse+ear&ie=utf-8&oe=utf-8
There's also the wikipedia entry http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vacanti_mouse
The more than a dozen pictures you get from a simple search https://www.google.com/search?q=mouse+ear&hl=en&client=ubuntu&hs=Gvo&channel=fs&prmd=imvns&source=lnms&tbm=isch&ei=FtRQT6TXCcf-4QTMh5nLDQ&sa=X&oi=mode_link&ct=mode&cd=2&ved=0CA4Q_AUoAQ&biw=1680&bih=829

Plus all manner of articles and essays and crap. Take a few and you can likely find a few scientific papers about it as well.

Also, as a cover story, something that gets intense attention from media and scientists (the people that per definition likes to prod things) well it's pretty much one of the worst I've ever imagined. If this really was the coverup, the foundation would need to explain the mouse in a way that works, then prove it to be repeatable in experimentation. Or more correctly, if they were going for a workable coverup they'd dismiss it as a hoax. Putting the Vacanti mouse on an article really breaks suspension of disbelif.

Also it's been around since 1997 or somesuch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dammit, I'm allergic to certain types of nuts. I wonder if I can get a refund, I've still got receipt.

+1 just as soon as I get the coffee wiped up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addendum Herp-Derp-17: On 12/4/20██, several photographs documenting Experiment 1103-2 were found leaked on the [REDACTED] Forums. A cover story claiming the photographs resulted from experimentation with embryonic stem cells was put into place and the remaining photographs were distributed to medical and scientific journals as well as major news agencies per standard procedure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
May contain traces of nuts!

I almost choked on some milk when I read that. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted at the first picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was just going to say it. I've seen it a number of times in scientific magazines and the like. It is somewhat famous, or at least common.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, you bastard. I just finished writing up a similar Dr. Wondertainment kit. I hate you so much.

(+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta give an upvote for Wondertainment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, sure, they told you it was a "genetic experiment" - what were they supposed to do when the picture went public, admit it was a Dr. Wondertainment kit experiment?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, i'm not sure. The first pic was indeed what inspired this… but then, I first saw the rat pic today ,though I knew it's been done.
If more people express the same sentiment, i'lll purge it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You might want to get rid of the second picture, that's a pretty well-known genetic experiment that totally ruins the article for me. The first picture, however, is brilliant, and Wondertainment gets an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That creeped the shit out of me

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The pictures… *shudder* Upvoted without hesitation.
